name: messages
title: Messages
description: One message within a thread: direction, the address it came from, its body, and the deferred-transport linkage fields (in_reply_to, external_message_id) a future IMAP sync would populate. A faithful port of a private predecessor-system audit's Message model, not part of this repo -- that source stored "content + external email fields/headers/direction" on one row; this schema keeps that same grain (no separate body_html field -- the audit gave no evidence the source split html from text at this level of detail, so this package carries one body_text field rather than inventing a split it can't confirm). "storage": "append" because a message, once sent or received, is (mostly) a write-once log entry -- the only field that ever flips after creation is is_read -- the same profile that put app-thread's thread_comments and app-catalog's stock_moves on append storage (docs/append-only-storage-design.md): write-hot, rarely-if-ever content-edited, an inspectable history in the raw file. Per-recipient To/Cc/Bcc addressing is normalized out to message_recipients, not flattened onto this row -- the source system normalized recipients into their own MessageRecipient table too (see schemas/message_recipients.json), and this package follows that same split rather than the alternative of flattening address lists onto Message. forms.default is not rendered by any page in this package in v1 -- composing/sending a real message is deferred (see dbbasic-package.json) -- it is kept for schema-generator consistency and a future compose page, same as forum_topics carries ai_summary unwritten.

fields.name: direction
fields.label: Direction
fields.help: inbound = received mail (the deferred IMAP sync's future insertion path); outbound = sent by this mailbox's owner (the deferred SMTP send's future insertion path). v1 has no page that creates a message directly through either path -- see dbbasic-package.json's deferred list.

fields.name: subject
fields.label: Subject
fields.help: A message can carry its own subject distinct from the thread's (e.g. a "Re:" prefix on a reply), same as real email.

fields.name: is_read
fields.label: Read
fields.help: Same default reasoning as message_threads.is_read: v1 ships no inbound-transport insertion path, so a message created here by hand, seed, or draft save defaults to already-read. A future sync job is what should set this false on newly-synced inbound mail.

fields.name: sent_at
fields.label: Sent At
fields.help: Set for an outbound message once the deferred SMTP send exists. Carried as a field now (same posture as app-invoices' paid_at) even though nothing in this package writes it yet.

fields.name: received_at
fields.label: Received At
fields.help: Set for an inbound message once the deferred IMAP sync exists. Carried as a field now, not written by anything in this package yet.

fields.name: in_reply_to
fields.label: In Reply To
fields.help: The external mail system's Message-ID this message replies to (RFC 5322 shape), for the deferred IMAP threading linkage. Carried, not validated or resolved by anything here.

fields.name: external_message_id
fields.label: External Message ID
fields.help: This message's identity in the external mail system (IMAP/mailcow) once the deferred sync exists. Carried, not populated by anything in this package.
